elements_applied.reference: Full-width headline text styled as poster typography, using Anton at 4.5rem with heavy rule underneath - mirrors OFFF's graphic poster announcements
Oversized semi-transparent page numbers (7rem) positioned as background watermarks in each view - inspired by OFFF's bold numeral hierarchy on event posters
Navigation as circled numbered stamps resembling postal/event admission stamps - draws from OFFF's ticketing and badge visual language
Rotated ink-stamp labels (URGENT, REVIEW, DRAFT) on idea cards - poster paste-up aesthetic with slight rotation transform
Intentionally irregular multi-column grid (1fr/1.1fr/0.9fr) creating asymmetric poster composition - not a uniform grid
Compact timestamp + description feed layout - directly inspired by NTS's show schedule with time codes and brief descriptions in dense rows
Dense, bordered, stacked task cards in kanban columns - modeled after NTS's compact show listings with minimal whitespace and utility metadata
IBM Plex Mono used extensively for labels, timestamps, badges, and field specs - echoes NTS's utility-first typographic approach
Volume/date metadata line in the masthead (VOL. I / 15 FEB 2026) - inspired by NTS's broadcast date and show numbering system
Dense horizontal tool strip with minimal-height stamp buttons - inspired by NTS's compact player controls and utility interface
Anton at 2.8rem with 8px letter-spacing as the app's identity - words functioning as visual interface element, not just label
Typographic pull-quotes with oversized opening quotation marks (4rem) and italic body text - editorial display typography as content hierarchy device
Section symbols and em-dashes as structural markers (section 1, section 2) in settings - typography as organizational system, not decoration
Kanban column headers styled as newspaper section mastheads with numero symbols and inverted color blocks - display type defining content zones
Dashboard numbers rendered at heading-xl scale (4.5rem) making data the visual focal point - inspired by Awwwards' data-as-display approach
Table of contents with dot leaders connecting file names to page references - classic typographic convention elevated as interface pattern

cross_reference_summary.coverage: All 10 views incorporate at least one traced inspiration element. No generic/unstyled components exist.
